# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build an Elasticsearch snapshot configuration for automated backups. Configure snapshot repository to S3 or file share for the operations team. Implement snapshot creation and restoration functionality.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent datastore security.
# Category: datastore_security

# This is a YAML code snippet that represents an Elasticsearch configuration with S3 as backup repository for automated backups, 
# snapshot creation and restoration functionality which are implemented using platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent datastore security.
elasticsearch_configs:
    clusterName: "yourCluster" # replace this value by your actual Cluster Name in ElasticSearch setup  
    
snapshotRepositoryRoles: 
 - name: snapshot1Role
   indices: ["*"]
   repositories: [{'type': 's3', 'enabled' : true, 'settings': {'bucket': 'yourBucketName','region': 'us-west-2'}, "policy": {"rules" : {".read_only":"deny", ".verify":"allow",".*":{"allow":["write"]}} }}]
    # replace these values by your actual bucket name and region in S3 
    
snapshotRepository: snapshot1Role  
# This line is used to define the role for snapshots. It's a reference back to our 'snapshot_repository'. If this was not defined, Elasticsearch would fail when trying restore operations with non-existing repository or no repositories at all 
    
restore:    # Define your restores here  
 - name: "myRestore"     
   snapshotRepositoryRoleName : 'snapshot1'
